content.verdict: Since 1932, the Alvear Palace has been Buenos Aires' grande dame — a Louis XV-styled landmark on Avenida Alvear where Argentine old-money society still takes its afternoon tea. The 197-room property marries Empire-style silver, hand-knotted French tapestries, and crystal chandeliers with 24-hour butler service and a discreetly contemporary spa. For couples drawn to Parisian-feel elegance, classical opulence, and refined formal service, no other Buenos Aires hotel comes closer to the feel of a privately owned European palace.
content.best_room: Book a Junior Suite on a high floor overlooking Avenida Alvear for classic parquet floors, Louis XV writing desks, and marble bathrooms with heated floors. The Alvear Suites add a separate sitting room ideal for evening Champagne service, and the legendary Presidential Suite sits on the top floor with a private terrace and panoramic Recoleta views — reserved for once-in-a-lifetime celebrations.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival & Afternoon Tea
itinerary_7_nights.description: Private transfer from Ezeiza International, welcome Champagne in your suite, and — if you arrive in the afternoon — a first pot of Alvear's famous afternoon tea in L'Orangerie, the glass-roofed gallery where three generations of Porteños have been celebrating birthdays and anniversaries.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Recoleta Walk
itinerary_7_nights.description: After breakfast, the concierge arranges a private walking tour of Recoleta Cemetery, Centro Cultural Recoleta, and the leafy Plaza Francia. Lunch at a neighbourhood confitería, then an afternoon at Museo Nacional de Bellas Artes. Evening: cocktails at the Oak Room piano bar followed by dinner at La Bourgogne, the hotel's Relais & Châteaux restaurant.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Tango & San Telmo
itinerary_7_nights.description: Late breakfast, then a guided afternoon through San Telmo's cobbled streets and antique markets. Return to dress for dinner and a late-night show at Café de los Angelitos — the hotel arranges prime seating and private car transfers.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Palermo Parks & MALBA
itinerary_7_nights.description: Spend the day exploring Palermo's parks, Rose Garden, Japanese Garden, and the MALBA museum of Latin American modern art. Lunch at Proper in Palermo Hollywood, then return for a couples' massage at the Alvear Spa before a quiet room-service supper.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Tigre Delta or Colonia
itinerary_7_nights.description: Choose a full-day ferry to UNESCO-listed Colonia del Sacramento in Uruguay or a private guided tour of the Tigre Delta's wooden stilt houses and mangrove channels. The hotel packs a picnic and arranges all transport door-to-door.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Alvear Art Gallery & Shopping
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning at the Alvear Art Gallery on the hotel's ground floor, then a leisurely browse of antique silver shops and ateliers along Avenida Alvear and Arroyo. Lunch at La Brigada for a true porteño parrilla experience. A long afternoon nap, then a farewell-eve dinner in your suite.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell Morning
itinerary_7_nights.description: A final breakfast in L'Orangerie, a last unhurried walk along Avenida Alvear's embassy row, and a farewell Champagne at Lobby Bar before private transfer to the airport or your onward Mendoza or Patagonia leg.

caveats: The Alvear aesthetic is unabashedly classical — if your design taste is modern or minimalist you may find the gilt, tapestries, and Louis XV detailing overwhelming.
The pool and spa are excellent but compact by resort standards; couples expecting a lavish resort wellness programme should set expectations accordingly.
Buenos Aires traffic, especially during rush hour on nearby Avenida 9 de Julio, can make outbound excursions slow — leave early, and lean on the hotel's driver team for timing.

faqs.question: Is the Alvear Palace a good honeymoon choice?
faqs.answer: Yes, particularly for couples who prize classical, European-feel grandeur, impeccable formal service, and a genuinely historic Recoleta address. The 24-hour butler team, L'Orangerie tea, and La Bourgogne fine dining are a honeymoon pairing few hotels in the Americas can match.

faqs.question: How does it compare with Palacio Duhau?
faqs.answer: Both are in Recoleta within a short walk of each other. Alvear feels more European-classical with a long-standing society clientele. Palacio Duhau combines palace rooms with a modern tower and slightly larger gardens. Alvear tends to be quieter and more formal; Duhau is livelier in public spaces.

faqs.question: What is L'Orangerie afternoon tea like?
faqs.answer: It is the Buenos Aires afternoon-tea benchmark: a tiered tower of scones, finger sandwiches, pastries, and miniatures served with tea or Champagne under a glass ceiling. Reserve a week ahead for weekend tea, especially if you want the corner window tables.

faqs.question: Is it adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, children are welcome, but the hotel's formal tone and clientele skew strongly adult. Honeymooners rarely encounter family-holiday energy in the public spaces.

faqs.question: What should we pre-book?
faqs.answer: L'Orangerie tea, La Bourgogne dinner, an in-suite couples' massage on arrival, Café de los Angelitos tango show, and any Colonia ferry tickets. Also ask the concierge about a private Teatro Colón backstage tour if opera interests you.

faqs.question: Can we pay in USD?
faqs.answer: Yes, the hotel settles in USD and accepts all major cards. Cash USD is useful for taxis, tips, and small purchases outside the hotel; the concierge can explain the practicalities of current exchange options.
